projectDescription: A beautiful, highly customizable Home Assistant Lovelace calendar card with multi-calendar support, multiple view modes, event management, weather integration, and rich styling options.
keyFeatures: Four view modes: Month, Week (compact), Schedule, and Agenda
Multi-calendar support with per-calendar color coding
Full event management: create, edit, and delete events
Weather forecast display via Home Assistant weather entities
Virtual calendars to group entities into display-only badge groups
Rich event styling engine with rule-based event_styles and day_styles
Day badges for custom conditional indicators on calendar days
Combine calendars feature to deduplicate events across multiple calendars
Dark mode support (auto, light, or dark)
Compact height mode for grid/panel dashboard layouts
Markdown and HTML support in event descriptions
Eight supported UI languages: English, French, German, Dutch, Spanish, Catalan, Danish, Swedish
Visual configuration editor (no YAML required for common options)
publicApiSurface: entities: list of calendar entity IDs
default_view: month | week | week-compact | week-standard | schedule | agenda
colors: map of calendar entity to #hex
event_font_colors: map of calendar entity to #hex
calendar_names: map of calendar entity to display name
calendar_badge_icons: map of calendar entity to mdi:icon
calendar_person_entities: map of calendar entity to person.* entity
background_image_position: center | top | bottom | left | right
readonly_calendars: list of calendar entity IDs
default_hidden_calendars: list of calendar entity IDs
hide_badge_calendars: list of calendar entity IDs
hide_times_for_calendars: list of calendar entity IDs
language: en | fr | de | nl | es | ca | da | sv
event_styles: list of rule objects with match/style/priority
day_styles: list of rule objects with condition/style/priority
day_badges: list of badge objects with conditions/text/icon/background_color
virtual_calendars: list of virtual calendar objects with id/name/entities/color/icon
uix: object with style CSS string
